topic: File Write/Edit Tools in AI Coding Assistants

gaps: Windsurf internal edit mechanism is proprietary/undocumented
Google Jules internal architecture not publicly available
OpenCode archived before tools were fully documented
Amazon Q Developer agentic editing details sparse
No direct performance benchmarks comparing all tools against each other

sources.title: Claude Code Tools Reference
keyInsights: Edit tool for targeted search-replace, Write for create/overwrite, Read for inspection
Permission system: Edit and Write require permission, Read does not
NotebookEdit for Jupyter notebooks
LSP integration reports errors automatically after file edits

sources.title: How Claude Code Works - Agentic Loop and Tools
keyInsights: Five tool categories: file operations, search, execution, web, code intelligence
Agentic loop: gather context, take action, verify results
Checkpoints created per-prompt before file changes
Read-before-Write requirement for Write tool

sources.title: Claude Code Checkpointing System
keyInsights: Every user prompt creates a new checkpoint
Checkpoints persist across sessions (30-day default retention)
Rewind menu: restore code, conversation, or both
Does NOT track changes made by Bash commands (rm, mv, cp)
Summarize from here option for targeted context compression

sources.title: Claude Code Best Practices
keyInsights: Context window is the most important resource to manage
Hooks for deterministic post-edit actions (lint, format)
Plan mode for exploration before editing
Read-before-Write enforcement prevents destructive overwrites

sources.title: Cursor Instant Apply - Technical Deep Dive
keyInsights: Full-file rewrite chosen over diff because models struggle with line numbers
Fine-tuned Llama-3-70B achieves ~1000 tokens/sec with speculative edits
13x speedup over standard inference, 9x over GPT-4
Speculative decoding uses deterministic prediction (most output = input unchanged)
Only Claude Opus reliably generated Aider-style SEARCH/REPLACE diffs
80/20 mix of real apply examples and cmd-k prompts for training

sources.title: Aider Edit Formats Documentation
keyInsights: Six edit formats: whole, diff, diff-fenced, udiff, editor-diff, editor-whole
diff-fenced created specifically for Gemini models
udiff reduced GPT-4 Turbo laziness
Editor formats for architect mode two-model approach

sources.title: Aider Unified Diffs - 3x Performance Improvement
keyInsights: Unified diffs tripled GPT-4 Turbo benchmark from 20% to 61%
Disabling flexible patching caused 9x increase in editing errors
High-level diff prompting reduced errors by 30-50%
Lazy comments dropped from 12 to 4 tasks with udiff
GPT acts more like writing data for a program than talking to a person

sources.title: Aider - Code in JSON Degrades Performance
keyInsights: ALL models performed worse with JSON-wrapped code
Claude 3.5 Sonnet and DeepSeek most severely impacted
JSON escaping causes syntax errors in generated code
Even without syntax errors, JSON cognitive burden reduces reasoning
OpenAI strict mode provided no improvement over standard JSON

sources.title: Aider Architect Mode - Two-Model Approach
keyInsights: Architect model describes solution, editor model formats edits
o1-preview + DeepSeek/o1-mini editor achieves 85% pass rate
Decouples reasoning ability from formatting compliance
Inspired by o1 models excelling at reasoning but struggling with edit formats

sources.title: Aider Edit Error Handling
keyInsights: Reports 'Failed to apply edit' on malformed edits
Makes every effort to handle 'almost' correctly formatted edits
Weaker models more prone to format violations
Recommends --edit-format whole or --architect for unreliable models

sources.title: Aider Commands - Undo and File Management
keyInsights: /undo reverts last aider-made git commit
Git-based undo mechanism
Files must be /add-ed to chat context before editing
/drop removes files to free context

sources.title: Aider SWE-Bench Lite Results
keyInsights: 26.3% on SWE-bench Lite (SOTA at time of publication)
Repo map via tree-sitter AST analysis, not RAG
Correctly identified target file in 70.3% of tasks
Multi-model strategy alternating GPT-4o and Claude Opus

sources.title: Codex CLI README - Sandbox and Approval Modes
keyInsights: Three modes: suggest (diffs), auto-edit (patches auto-applied), full-auto (complete autonomy)
macOS: Apple Seatbelt sandbox, Linux: Bubblewrap/Docker
Network blocked in sandbox except OpenAI API
Warns on non-git directories

sources.title: Codex apply-patch Rust Implementation
keyInsights: Custom patch format: *** Begin Patch / *** End Patch
Three operations: Add File, Update File, Delete File
Fuzzy matching with Unicode normalization via seek_sequence
Replacements applied in reverse order to preserve indices
Supports file move/rename within patches

sources.title: Codex apply_patch Tool Instructions
keyInsights: Exact patch format specification with Begin/End markers
@@ headers with optional context (class/function names)
3 lines of context before/after changes by default
File paths must be relative, never absolute
Multiple @@ statements for nested context disambiguation

sources.title: Codex Rust Core - Sandbox Architecture
keyInsights: Platform-specific sandboxing: Seatbelt (macOS), Bubblewrap (Linux), elevated backends (Windows)
.git directories protected as read-only
Binary convention: codex-linux-sandbox for sandbox ops, --codex-run-as-apply-patch for patches
Complex carveouts auto-route through bubblewrap over legacy enforcement

sources.title: Cline diff.ts - Tiered Matching Implementation
keyInsights: Three-tier fallback: exact match, line-trimmed, block anchor
SEARCH/REPLACE format with ------- SEARCH / ======= / +++++++ REPLACE markers
Empty SEARCH = entire file replacement
Streaming-compatible: processes chunks sequentially
Handles out-of-order edits and partial markers at chunk boundaries

sources.title: Roo Code apply_diff Tool Documentation
keyInsights: Fuzzy matching via Levenshtein distance on normalized strings
start_line hint guides middle-out search within BUFFER_LINES window (default 40)
Configurable confidence threshold (0.8-1.0)
consecutiveMistakeCountForApplyDiff tracks repeated failures
<<<<<<< SEARCH / ======= / >>>>>>> REPLACE format with mandatory :start_line:

sources.title: Roo Code write_to_file Tool Documentation
keyInsights: Parameters: path, content, line_count (for truncation detection)
Much slower and less efficient than apply_diff for modifications
Checks .rooignore and workspace boundaries
Preprocesses to remove code block markers and escaped HTML
Interactive approval required before write

sources.title: Continue.dev Apply Model Role Documentation
keyInsights: Dedicated apply role generates precise diffs from chat suggestions
Recommended: Morph Fast Apply, Relace Instant Apply (free tiers)
Handlebars templates: {{{original_code}}} and {{{new_code}}}
Bridges gap when chat/edit outputs don't align with existing code

sources.title: Continue.dev Deterministic Apply - AST-Based
keyInsights: AST-based matching using tree-sitter parsers
Handles lazy blocks ('... existing code ...') via node matching
Myers diff-like algorithm for lazy block consumption
Rejects diffs with >30% removals (REMOVAL_PERCENTAGE_THRESHOLD)
Falls back to LLM-based apply for uncertain transformations

sources.title: Windsurf Cascade Documentation
keyInsights: Code mode: direct file creation/modification; Chat mode: proposal-based
Automatic linter fix on generated code (free of credit charge)
Checkpoint/revert system (currently irreversible reverts)
Uses Search, Analyze, Web Search, MCP, terminal tools

sources.title: Google Gemini Code Assist Overview
keyInsights: Inline code completions, full block generation from comments
Smart actions via right-click context menu
Chat-based modification requests
All suggestions require developer review and validation

sources.title: Amazon Q Developer Code Transformation
keyInsights: Three-stage: build, transform, verify
Minimal changes necessary philosophy
Diff view review before application
Supports dependency upgrade YAML specification
JetBrains module-based, VS Code project-based

sources.title: OpenCode Repository (Archived)
keyInsights: Go-based terminal AI assistant with TUI (Bubble Tea)
File searching, modification, command execution
LSP and MCP integration
SQLite session storage
Now archived, succeeded by Charm's Crush

sources.title: OpenCode Website
keyInsights: 120K+ GitHub stars, 800+ contributors
LSP integration: automatically loads right LSPs
Parallel agents on same project
Privacy-first: does not store code or context data

sources.title: Roo Code Repository
keyInsights: VS Code extension, TypeScript-based (98.7%)
Five modes: Code, Architect, Ask, Debug, Custom
Human-in-the-loop: every modification requires approval
Monorepo structure with pnpm workspace

sources.title: Cline Repository
keyInsights: VS Code extension for AI-assisted development
Diff view for review before changes applied
Error monitoring: tracks linter/compiler errors proactively
Changes recorded in VS Code Timeline for rollback

sources.title: Cursor Agent Mode Documentation
keyInsights: No limit on tool calls per task
Automatic checkpoints before significant changes
Tools: semantic search, file/folder search, shell, browser, image generation
Model-specific tuning of instructions and tools

sources.title: Claude Code Overview
keyInsights: Available in terminal, IDE, desktop, browser, web
Hooks for auto-formatting after file edits
Skills for reusable workflows
Subagents for isolated task delegation

sources.title: Sourcegraph - Lifecycle of AI Code Completion
keyInsights: Four-stage pipeline: planning, retrieval, generation, post-processing
Tree-sitter for syntax validation and truncation
Levenshtein edit distances for duplicate detection
Fill-in-the-middle (infilling) with prefix+suffix context
End-to-end latency must be under 1 second

sources.title: LLM Code Generation Workflow Comparison
keyInsights: Manual copy-paste (Claude.ai) vs automated application (Aider)
Aider handles testing automatically
Structured planning produces discrete executable chunks
repomix for context bundling in established codebases

sources.title: Claude Code Documentation Index
keyInsights: Full documentation structure with 60+ pages
Tools reference, checkpointing, hooks, skills, subagents documented
Separate pages for permissions, sandboxing, security

sources.title: OpenAI Codex Repository
keyInsights: Two implementations: codex-cli (TypeScript) and codex-rs (Rust)
Open source coding agent from OpenAI
Available via npm and Homebrew

sources.title: Continue.dev Repository
keyInsights: Source-controlled AI checks on pull requests
Generates diffs as suggestions, not autonomous changes
Markdown-based check configuration in .continue/checks/
CLI tool (cn command) for automation

sources.title: Amazon Q Developer Overview
keyInsights: IDE integrations: VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio, Eclipse
Chat, inline completions, code generation, security scanning
Also available in AWS Console, Slack, Teams

sources.title: Claude Code Hooks Guide (referenced)
keyInsights: Shell commands run before/after tool execution
Auto-format after file edits
Block writes to protected directories
Deterministic unlike CLAUDE.md instructions which are advisory

sources.title: Claude Code Sandboxing (referenced)
keyInsights: Filesystem and network isolation
OS-level sandbox for full-auto workflows
Complements permission system

sources.title: Roo Code Wiki - Tools
keyInsights: Wiki pages reference tool documentation
Multiple tool categories available
Community-maintained documentation

sources.title: Cline Wiki
keyInsights: AI assistant for VSCode using CLI and editor
Git checkpoints for safety
Shell integration documentation referenced

sources.title: Simon Willison on OpenAI Codex
keyInsights: Codex CLI described as 'same shape as Claude Code'
Agentic Codex runs in network-disconnected sandbox during agent loop
Returns results as diffs after 8-10 minutes
